content.claim: Hungary's 6- and 8-year gimnázium tracks were introduced in the early 1990s as post-1990 policy additions layered onto an inherited 8-grade comprehensive system, not ancient institutions, which affects feasibility by allowing both selective tracks and comprehensive defaults to be framed as 'status quo' by different actors.
content.context: Fresh live research through the Political feasibility lens
limitations: This is a descriptive fact about institutional history, not a causal claim about contemporary support or reversal risk
The political implication that framing flexibility aids reform assumes actors will successfully exploit it, which is not guaranteed
content.population: Population described in the cited finding
content.time_scope: As reported by the cited source
